ops.title: FIXED in v0.27.0 — ship resolves the push target pre-commit (local-only, refusal mutates nothing) and refuses empty @ (parents>1 exempt); the old strand-then-empty-duplicate workaround is obsolete; session traps: cc-skills is jj-colocated, codex plan-ref
ops.body:
The quirk ("ship can fail trunk resolution after forming the commit; --bookmark retry pushes it plus a harmless empty duplicate tip; leave it, never force-push") is FIXED as of ccx v0.27.0 (88e4dd0803e9, 2026-07-17) — the workaround guidance is obsolete. Current behavior:

- `shipPreflight{,JJ,Git}` run every resolution refusal (multi-bookmark trunk, non-trunk nearest, `--bookmark` misses, detached HEAD) **before the commit forms**, local-only — a refused ship leaves the working copy AND the jj op log byte-identical. Fetch/divergence/rebase stay post-commit in the push phase, unchanged from before.
- An empty jj `@` refuses: `ship: nothing to commit — did a prior ship already land <sha> "<subject>"? push it: jj bookmark move exact:<t> --to @- && jj git push --bookmark exact:<t>` (hint only when pre-flight validated a target; task 1f46157 closed). Exempt: `--amend`, hunk selections, merges (`parents > 1` — which also covers conflicted empty merges; conflict alone is NOT exempt or conflicted stacks ship unlimited empty duplicates).
- Behavior delta worth knowing: parked on a tracked feature bookmark whose remote advanced, ship now refuses (pass `--bookmark feature`) where it previously would silently advance trunk onto the feature-based stack.

Traps hit this session:

- **cc-skills is jj-colocated** — a raw `git commit`+`git push origin main` there lands on a detached HEAD and pushes nothing ("Everything up-to-date" lies). Recover: `jj bookmark move exact:main --to <sha>` + `jj git push --bookmark exact:main`. Same genus as [[jj-prek-commit-corruption]].
- **Codex mirrors the repo's plan-re-entry convention**: a big implementation brief can come back as a plan awaiting approval instead of edits. Re-dispatch with "APPROVED — implement now" pointing at its own reply file on disk; don't re-send the spec.
- **jj `description("x")` / `description(exact:"x")` won't match** a committed description's trailing newline in scratch scripts — anchor scratch scenarios with bookmarks (`jj bookmark create`) instead of description revsets.
- **codex-ask reply files are created empty up-front** — an empty `codex-r-*` means still-running, not failed; only non-empty content is a completed reply. Related: [[ship-auto-rebase]] (background-wait trap).
